goal: Calibrate Franka Panda sim actuator model against a real-robot HDF5 recording. quick_calibrate fits armature, Coulomb friction, and link masses (~5 min). validate_calibration checks RMSE on a held-out trajectory. If RMSE > 0.01 rad, create_calibration_experiment sweeps the joint_damping axis to close the gap. get_drive_gains reads the post-calibration drive state; set_drive_gains applies tuned Kp/Kd. measure_sim_real_gap produces a final gap report; suggest_parameter_adjustment flags remaining residuals. SUCCESS: validate_calibration RMSE < 0.01 rad on all 7 joints. Exercises physics calibration pipeline (quick_calibrate, validate_calibration, create_calibration_experiment, measure_sim_real_gap, suggest_parameter_adjustment). Realizes sim-to-real / RL engineer persona.
thoughts: Pattern is rl-train / physics-calibration. No cube delivery criterion — success is numerical RMSE threshold, not a prim-at-destination check. simulate_args and verify_args are null accordingly.

quick_calibrate is chosen over calibrate_physics for this canonical: the full Bayesian-optimization job (30-120 min) is impractical in an interactive session. quick_calibrate covers the 3 highest-impact parameters (armature, friction, masses) in ~5 min.
Real data is assumed to reside at workspace/calibration/franka_real_excitation.hdf5. The canonical uses this path consistently — caller must substitute their own path.

validate_calibration uses a separate held-out file (franka_real_holdout.hdf5) to avoid reporting in-sample overfitting. baseline_error is omitted on the first call to get the absolute calibrated RMSE.
create_calibration_experiment is triggered only on RMSE > threshold — the canonical models the conditional branch in its code with a comment noting that the sweep is run manually if the guard fires.

get_drive_gains reads the current Kp/Kd before any update; set_drive_gains applies recommended values from calibration. Franka defaults: Kp=6000, Kd=500 — overridden only if calibration explicitly diverges.
measure_sim_real_gap produces a structured gap report that suggest_parameter_adjustment then annotates. Together they give the human operator a prioritized remediation list.
motion_controllers.untested contains direct_joint because the calibration workflow replays open-loop joint trajectories, not a closed-loop planner. No cuRobo or RMPflow involved.

Distinct from CP-NEW-dr-curriculum-trainer (DR for training-distribution), CP-NEW-rl-clone-env (PPO env scaffold), and CP-NEW-machine-tender-door-interlock (safety stop). This is the ONLY canonical that exercises the physics-calibration pipeline end-to-end.
code:
# ── Load Franka articulation ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
robot_wizard(
    robot_name="franka",
    dest_path="/World/Franka",
    position=[0, 0, 0.0],
    orientation=[1, 0, 0, 0],
)

# ── Phase 1: Quick calibration (~5 min) ──────────────────────────────────────
# Fits armature (rotor inertia), Coulomb friction, and link masses.
_calib_result = quick_calibrate(
    real_data_path="workspace/calibration/franka_real_excitation.hdf5",
    articulation_path="/World/Franka",
    include_masses=True,
    output_dir="workspace/calibration/franka_quick",
)
# Returns: {calibrated_parameters: {armature, joint_friction, link_masses}, rmse_train: float}

# ── Phase 2: Validate on held-out trajectory ──────────────────────────────────
_val_result = validate_calibration(
    calibrated_params=_calib_result["calibrated_parameters"],
    test_data_path="workspace/calibration/franka_real_holdout.hdf5",
)
# Returns: {per_joint_rmse: {joint_0..6: float}, mean_rmse: float, ee_cartesian_rmse: float}
# SUCCESS criterion: mean_rmse < 0.01 rad

# ── Phase 3 (conditional): Sweep joint_damping if RMSE too high ──────────────
# Trigger this sweep manually if _val_result["mean_rmse"] > 0.01.
create_calibration_experiment(
    parameter="damping",
    range=[0.1, 2.0],
    real_data_path="workspace/calibration/franka_real_holdout.hdf5",
    num_samples=7,
)
# Returns: {best_value: float, sweep_results: [{param_value, rmse}]} — pick best_value for set_drive_gains.

# ── Phase 4: Read and apply tuned drive gains ─────────────────────────────────
_current_gains = get_drive_gains(
    joint_path="/World/Franka/panda_joint1",
)
# Returns: {angular: {stiffness: float, damping: float}, linear: {stiffness: float, damping: float}}

# Apply calibration-recommended Kp/Kd on all 7 Franka joints
for _jidx in range(7):
    set_drive_gains(
        joint_path=f"/World/Franka/panda_joint{_jidx + 1}",
        kp=6000.0,
        kd=500.0,
    )

# ── Phase 5: Quantify final sim-to-real gap ───────────────────────────────────
_gap_report = measure_sim_real_gap(
    sim_trajectory="workspace/calibration/franka_sim_replay.hdf5",
    real_trajectory="workspace/calibration/franka_real_holdout.hdf5",
)
# Returns: {per_joint_errors: {}, ee_cartesian_error: float, obs_distribution_gap: {}}

# ── Phase 6: Prioritized remediation list ────────────────────────────────────
_adjustments = suggest_parameter_adjustment(
    gap_report=_gap_report,
)
# Returns: [{parameter, direction, magnitude, priority}] — human operator applies top-N.
verified_status: drafted-2026-05-16; form-gate ✓; function-gate BLOCKED (requires real HDF5 robot logs at workspace/calibration/; quick_calibrate + validate_calibration need IsaacSim physics backend running)
failure_modes: quick_calibrate: HDF5 file must have columns joint_positions, joint_velocities, joint_torques_commanded at 200-500 Hz; if column names differ, the handler raises a KeyError — pre-inspect with h5py before calling
validate_calibration calibrated_params key mismatch: quick_calibrate returns 'calibrated_parameters' dict — pass this sub-dict, not the outer response; wrong level produces a schema validation error
create_calibration_experiment range=[0.1, 2.0] for damping: too wide a range may produce physically unstable sim steps (high damping → stiff ODEs); narrow to [0.2, 1.0] if the sim diverges mid-sweep

set_drive_gains loop: /World/Franka/panda_joint{n} path format assumes the Franka USD uses panda_joint1..7 naming; Nova Carter or alternate Franka variants may differ — verify with list_attributes first

measure_sim_real_gap: sim_trajectory must be generated by replaying the same excitation sequence in sim after calibration; if not regenerated, the gap will reflect pre-calibration state and mislead suggest_parameter_adjustment
suggest_parameter_adjustment: gap_report may contain obs_distribution_gap with NaN if the sim replay was too short; ensure sim replay covers at least 30s of excitation to match holdout duration
extension_notes: First canonical to exercise the physics-calibration pipeline (quick_calibrate + validate_calibration + create_calibration_experiment + measure_sim_real_gap + suggest_parameter_adjustment). Distinct from all existing pick-place and DR canonicals — no delivery, no conveyor, no policy training. Pure sim-to-real gap identification and remediation workflow.
code_template:
robot_wizard(
    robot_name={{primary_robot.class}},
    dest_path={{primary_robot.path}},
    position={{primary_robot.position}},
    orientation={{primary_robot.orientation}},
)

_calib_result = quick_calibrate(
    real_data_path={{calib_data_path}},
    articulation_path={{primary_robot.path}},
    include_masses=True,
    output_dir={{calib_output_dir}},
)

_val_result = validate_calibration(
    calibrated_params=_calib_result["calibrated_parameters"],
    test_data_path={{holdout_data_path}},
)

create_calibration_experiment(
    parameter="damping",
    range=[0.1, 2.0],
    real_data_path={{holdout_data_path}},
    num_samples=7,
)

_current_gains = get_drive_gains(
    joint_path={{primary_robot.path}} + "/panda_joint1",
)

for _jidx in range(7):
    set_drive_gains(
        joint_path=f"{{primary_robot.path}}/panda_joint{_jidx + 1}",
        kp=6000.0,
        kd=500.0,
    )

_gap_report = measure_sim_real_gap(
    sim_trajectory={{sim_traj_path}},
    real_trajectory={{holdout_data_path}},
)

_adjustments = suggest_parameter_adjustment(
    gap_report=_gap_report,
)
